RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 109-C, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Rep. Keri Ingle, a Democrat, represents District 35 which includes Lee’s Summit, Kansas City, and Raytown in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2018.
Rep. Ingle earned a BA in Criminal Justice from the University of Central Oklahoma, and a Masters in Social Work from the University of Kansas. Rep. Ingle is a Social Worker and has worked in child welfare as the adoption specialist for the Jackson County Children’s Division, as well as in schools, hospitals, and mental health facilities. Ingle was awarded the National Association of Social Workers Elected Official of the Year for Missouri in 2021, and the Missouri Kids Win Legislator of the Year in 2022.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires the department of health and senior services to promulgate regulations consistent with CDC guidelines for prescribing opioids
Prohibits certain mental health professionals from engaging in conversion therapy with minors
Changes the laws regarding the dispensing of contraceptives
Establishes the position of dementia services coordinator within the department of health and senior services
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to firearms
Proposes a constitutional amendment establishing crime victims' rights to a civil action with the same time limitations as the criminal action
